  289: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  290: // Git operations
  291: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  292: 
  293: export function gitCommitAndPush(
  294:   config: Pick<Config, 'auto_push'>,
  295:   projectPath: string,
  296:   projectName: string,
  297:   count: number,
  298: ): void {
  299:   const logger = getLogger()
  300: 
  301:   const changedPaths = changedMemoryPaths(projectPath)
  302:   if (changedPaths.length === 0) {
  303:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] No git changes in Memory/.`)
  304:     return
  305:   }
  306: 
  307:   const stageablePaths = changedPaths.filter(path => !isRepoRawTranscriptPath(path))
  308:   if (stageablePaths.length === 0) {
  309:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] Only raw transcript mirror changes detected; skipping commit.`)
  310:     return
  311:   }
  312: 
  313:   git(projectPath, 'add', '--', ...stageablePaths)
  314:   git(projectPath, 'commit', '-m', `memorytree(transcripts): import ${count} transcript(s)`)
  315:   logger.info(`[${projectName}] Committed ${count} transcript import(s).`)
  316: 
  317:   if (!config.auto_push) {
  318:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] auto_push disabled, skipping push.`)
  319:     return
  320:   }
  321: 
  322:   const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
  323:   if (!remotes.trim()) {
  324:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] No git remote configured, skipping push.`)
  325:     writeAlert(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'no_remote', 'Push skipped: no Git remote configured.')
  326:     return
  327:   }
  328: 
  329:   if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
  330:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Push failed, retrying once...`)
  331:     if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
  332:       logger.error(`[${projectName}] Push failed after retry.`)
  333:       writeAlertWithThreshold(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed', 'Push failed after retry.')
  334:       return
  335:     }
  336:   }
  337: 
  338:   resetFailureCount(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed')
  339: }
  340: 
  341: export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
  342:   try {
  343:     const branch = currentBranch(projectPath)
  344:     if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
  345:       git(projectPath, 'push')
  346:     } else {
  347:       const upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath, branch)
  348:       if (upstream.remote === null) {
  349:         return false
  350:       }
  351:       getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Configured upstream ${upstream.remote}/${branch}.`)
  352:     }
  353:     getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
  354:     return true
  355:   } catch {
  356:     return false
  357:   }
  358: }
  359: 
  360: export function currentBranch(projectPath: string): string {
  361:   return git(projectPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
  362: }
  363: 
  364: export function isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch(
  365:   branch: string,
  366:   project?: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>,
  367: ): boolean {
  368:   return isProjectMemoryBranch(branch, project)
  369: }
  370: 
  371: export function changedMemoryPaths(projectPath: string): string[] {
  372:   const status = git(projectPath, 'status', '--porcelain', '--untracked-files=all', '--', 'Memory/')
  373:   const seen = new Set<string>()
  374:   const paths: string[] = []
  375: 
  376:   for (const rawLine of status.split(/\r?\n/)) {
  377:     const path = statusLinePath(rawLine)
  378:     if (path === null || seen.has(path)) continue
  379:     seen.add(path)
  380:     paths.push(path)
  381:   }
  382: 
  383:   return paths
  384: }
  385: 
  386: export function isRepoRawTranscriptPath(path: string): boolean {
  387:   return path.replace(/\\/g, '/').startsWith(RAW_TRANSCRIPT_PREFIX)
  388: }
  389: 
  390: function statusLinePath(line: string): string | null {
  391:   if (line.length < 4) return null
  392: 
  393:   const rawPath = line.slice(3).trim()
  394:   if (!rawPath) return null
  395: 
  396:   const path = rawPath.includes(' -> ')
  397:     ? rawPath.slice(rawPath.lastIndexOf(' -> ') + 4)
  398:     : rawPath
  399: 
  400:   return unquotePath(path)
  401: }
  402: 
  403: function unquotePath(path: string): string {
  404:   if (!(path.startsWith('"') && path.endsWith('"'))) return path
  405: 
  406:   return path
  407:     .slice(1, -1)
  408:     .replace(/\\"/g, '"')
  409:     .replace(/\\\\/g, '\\')
  410: }

  291: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  292: 
  293: export function gitCommitAndPush(
  294:   config: Pick<Config, 'auto_push'>,
  295:   projectPath: string,
  296:   projectName: string,
  297:   count: number,
  298: ): void {
  299:   const logger = getLogger()
  300: 
  301:   const changedPaths = changedMemoryPaths(projectPath)
  302:   if (changedPaths.length === 0) {
  303:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] No git changes in Memory/.`)
  304:     return
  305:   }
  306: 
  307:   const stageablePaths = changedPaths.filter(path => !isRepoRawTranscriptPath(path))
  308:   if (stageablePaths.length === 0) {
  309:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] Only raw transcript mirror changes detected; skipping commit.`)
  310:     return
  311:   }
  312: 
  313:   git(projectPath, 'add', '--', ...stageablePaths)
  314:   git(projectPath, 'commit', '-m', `memorytree(transcripts): import ${count} transcript(s)`)
  315:   logger.info(`[${projectName}] Committed ${count} transcript import(s).`)
  316: 
  317:   if (!config.auto_push) {
  318:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] auto_push disabled, skipping push.`)
  319:     return
  320:   }
  321: 
  322:   const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
  323:   if (!remotes.trim()) {
  324:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] No git remote configured, skipping push.`)
  325:     writeAlert(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'no_remote', 'Push skipped: no Git remote configured.')
  326:     return
  327:   }
  328: 
  329:   if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
  330:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Push failed, retrying once...`)
  331:     if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
  332:       logger.error(`[${projectName}] Push failed after retry.`)
  333:       writeAlertWithThreshold(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed', 'Push failed after retry.')
  334:       return
  335:     }
  336:   }
  337: 
  338:   resetFailureCount(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed')
  339: }
  340: 
  341: export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
  342:   try {
  343:     const branch = currentBranch(projectPath)
  344:     if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
  345:       git(projectPath, 'push')
  346:     } else {
  347:       const upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath, branch)
  348:       if (upstream.remote === null) {
  349:         return false
  350:       }
  351:       getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Configured upstream ${upstream.remote}/${branch}.`)
  352:     }
  353:     getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
  354:     return true
  355:   } catch {
  356:     return false
  357:   }
  358: }
  359: 
  360: export function currentBranch(projectPath: string): string {
  361:   return git(projectPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
  362: }
  363: 
  364: export function isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch(
  365:   branch: string,
  366:   project?: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>,
  367: ): boolean {
  368:   return isProjectMemoryBranch(branch, project)
  369: }
  370: 
  371: export function changedMemoryPaths(projectPath: string): string[] {
  372:   const status = git(projectPath, 'status', '--porcelain', '--untracked-files=all', '--', 'Memory/')
  373:   const seen = new Set<string>()
  374:   const paths: string[] = []
  375: 
  376:   for (const rawLine of status.split(/\r?\n/)) {
  377:     const path = statusLinePath(rawLine)
  378:     if (path === null || seen.has(path)) continue
  379:     seen.add(path)
  380:     paths.push(path)
  381:   }
  382: 
  383:   return paths
  384: }
  385: 
  386: export function isRepoRawTranscriptPath(path: string): boolean {
  387:   return path.replace(/\\/g, '/').startsWith(RAW_TRANSCRIPT_PREFIX)
  388: }
  389: 
  390: function statusLinePath(line: string): string | null {
  391:   if (line.length < 4) return null
  392: 
  393:   const rawPath = line.slice(3).trim()
  394:   if (!rawPath) return null
  395: 
  396:   const path = rawPath.includes(' -> ')
  397:     ? rawPath.slice(rawPath.lastIndexOf(' -> ') + 4)
  398:     : rawPath
  399: 
  400:   return unquotePath(path)
  401: }
  402: 
  403: function unquotePath(path: string): string {
  404:   if (!(path.startsWith('"') && path.endsWith('"'))) return path
  405: 
  406:   return path
  407:     .slice(1, -1)
  408:     .replace(/\\"/g, '"')
  409:     .replace(/\\\\/g, '\\')
  410: }

   30:   info: 1,
   31:   warn: 2,
   32:   error: 3,
   33: }
   34:

   36:   debug: 'DEBUG',
   37:   info: 'INFO',
   38:   warn: 'WARN',
   39:   error: 'ERROR',
   40: }
   41:

   46: let singleton: MemoryTreeLogger | undefined

   48: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   49: // MemoryTreeLogger
   50: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   51: 
   52: class MemoryTreeLogger implements Logger {
   53:   private readonly levelPriority: number
   54:   private readonly logFilePath: string | undefined
   55: 
   56:   constructor(logLevel: LogLevel) {
   57:     this.levelPriority = LEVEL_PRIORITY[logLevel]
   58: 
   59:     const filePath = logFilePathForToday()
   60:     const logDir = resolve(filePath, '..')
   61:     try {

   64:       // confirm access.  We store the path only after the dir exists.
   65:       this.logFilePath = filePath
   66:     } catch {

   68:       this.writeStderr('WARN', `Could not open log file: ${filePath}`)
   69:       this.logFilePath = undefined
   70:     }
   71:   }
   72: 
   73:   debug(msg: string): void {
   74:     this.log('debug', msg)
   75:   }
   76: 
   77:   info(msg: string): void {
   78:     this.log('info', msg)
   79:   }
   80: 
   81:   warn(msg: string): void {
   82:     this.log('warn', msg)
   83:   }
   84: 
   85:   error(msg: string): void {
   86:     this.log('error', msg)
   87:   }
   88: 
   89:   exception(msg: string, err?: unknown): void {
   90:     if (err instanceof Error && err.stack) {
   91:       this.log('error', `${msg}\n${err.stack}`)
   92:     } else if (err !== undefined) {
   93:       this.log('error', `${msg}\n${String(err)}`)
   94:     } else {

   96:     }
   97:   }
   98: 
   99:   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  100:   // Internal
  101:   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  102: 
  103:   private log(level: LogLevel, msg: string): void {
  104:     if (LEVEL_PRIORITY[level] < this.levelPriority) {
  105:       return
  106:     }
  107: 
  108:     const label = LEVEL_LABELS[level]
  109:     const formatted = `${timestamp()} [${label}] ${msg}`
  110: 
  111:     this.writeStderr(label, msg)
  112:     this.writeFile(formatted)
  113:   }
  114: 
  115:   private writeStderr(_label: string, _msg: string): void {
  116:     // Use the pre-formatted line for consistency.  We rebuild here so the
  117:     // standalone warning in the constructor can also call this without a
  118:     // full formatted line.
  119:     const line = `${timestamp()} [${_label}] ${_msg}\n`
  120:     process.stderr.write(line)
  121:   }

  124:     if (this.logFilePath === undefined) {
  125:       return
  126:     }

  129:     } catch {
  130:       // Silently ignore file-write errors 鈥?stderr is the primary channel.
  131:     }
  132:   }
  133: }
  134: 
  135: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  136: // Public API
  137: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  138: 
  139: /**
  140:  * Create (or return existing) singleton logger.
  141:  *
  142:  * The `logLevel` parameter is only used on the *first* call.  Subsequent
  143:  * calls return the existing logger regardless of the level argument.
  144:  */
  145: export function setupLogging(logLevel: LogLevel = 'info'): Logger {
  146:   if (singleton !== undefined) {
  147:     return singleton
  148:   }
  149:   singleton = new MemoryTreeLogger(resolveLevel(logLevel))
  150:   return singleton
  151: }
  152: 
  153: /**

  155:  * the logger is auto-created with default settings (`info` level).

  163:

  169: }
  170: 
  171: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  172: // Internal helpers
  173: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  174: 
  175: function resolveLevel(level: string): LogLevel {
  176:   const lower = level.toLowerCase()
  177:   const mapping: Record<string, LogLevel> = {
  178:     debug: 'debug',
  179:     info: 'info',
  180:     warn: 'warn',
  181:     warning: 'warn',
  182:     error: 'error',
  183:   }
  184:   return mapping[lower] ?? 'info'
  185: }
  186: 
  187: function logFilePathForToday(): string {
  188:   const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10) // YYYY-MM-DD in UTC
  189:   return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs', `heartbeat-${today}.log`)
  190: }
  191: 
  192: function timestamp(): string {
  193:   // Produce YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS in UTC matching the Python datefmt.
  194:   const now = new Date()
  195:   const y = now.getUTCFullYear()
  196:   const mo = String(now.getUTCMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')
  197:   const d = String(now.getUTCDate()).padStart(2, '0')
  198:   const h = String(now.getUTCHours()).padStart(2, '0')
  199:   const mi = String(now.getUTCMinutes()).padStart(2, '0')
  200:   const s = String(now.getUTCSeconds()).padStart(2, '0')
  201:   return `${y}-${mo}-${d}T${h}:${mi}:${s}`
  202: }

   44: export function cmdInstall(options: { interval?: string; autoPush?: string }): number {
   45:   let config = loadConfig()
   46: 
   47:   if (options.interval) {
   48:     config = { ...config, heartbeat_interval: options.interval }
   49:   }
   50:   if (options.autoPush) {
   51:     config = { ...config, auto_push: options.autoPush === 'true' }
   52:   }
   53: 
   54:   saveConfig(config)
   55:   const seconds = intervalToSeconds(config.heartbeat_interval)
   56:   const scriptPath = heartbeatScriptPath()
   57: 
   58:   const sys = platform
   59:   if (sys === 'linux') return installCron(scriptPath, seconds)
   60:   if (sys === 'darwin') return installLaunchd(scriptPath, seconds)
   61:   if (sys === 'win32') return installSchtasks(scriptPath, seconds)
   62: 
   63:   process.stderr.write(`Unsupported platform: ${sys}\n`)
   64:   return 1
   65: }
   66: 
   67: export function cmdUninstall(): number {
   68:   const sys = platform
   69:   if (sys === 'linux') return uninstallCron()
   70:   if (sys === 'darwin') return uninstallLaunchd()
   71:   if (sys === 'win32') return uninstallSchtasks()
   72: 
   73:   process.stderr.write(`Unsupported platform: ${sys}\n`)
   74:   return 1
   75: }
   76: 
   77: export async function cmdRunOnce(options: { root?: string; force?: boolean } = {}): Promise<number> {
   78:   const { main } = await import('../heartbeat/heartbeat.js')
   79:   return main(options)
   80: }
   81: 
   82: export async function cmdWatch(options: { interval?: string }): Promise<number> {
   83:   const config = loadConfig()
   84:   const intervalStr = options.interval ?? config.heartbeat_interval
   85:   const seconds = intervalToSeconds(intervalStr)
   86:   const { main } = await import('../heartbeat/heartbeat.js')
   87: 
   88:   process.stdout.write(`Watch mode: running heartbeat every ${seconds}s. Press Ctrl+C to stop.\n`)
   89:   try {
   90:     // eslint-disable-next-line no-constant-condition
   91:     while (true) {
   92:       await main()
   93:       await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, seconds * 1000))
   94:     }
   95:   } catch {
   96:     process.stdout.write('\nWatch mode stopped.\n')
   97:   }
   98:   return 0
   99: }
  100: 
  101: export function cmdStatus(): number {
  102:   const registered = isHeartbeatRegistered()
  103: 
  104:   const sys = platform
  105:   const platformName = sys === 'darwin' ? 'Darwin' : sys === 'win32' ? 'Windows' : 'Linux'
  106:   process.stdout.write(`Platform:   ${platformName}\n`)
  107:   process.stdout.write(`Registered: ${registered ? 'yes' : 'no'}\n`)
  108: 
  109:   const pid = readLockPid()
  110:   if (pid !== null) {
  111:     process.stdout.write(`Lock:       held by PID ${pid}\n`)
  112:   } else {
  113:     process.stdout.write('Lock:       not held\n')
  114:   }
  115: 
  116:   if (existsSync(configPath())) {
  117:     const config = loadConfig()
  118:     process.stdout.write(`Interval:   ${config.heartbeat_interval}\n`)
  119:     process.stdout.write(`Auto-push:  ${config.auto_push}\n`)
  120:     process.stdout.write(`Projects:   ${config.projects.length}\n`)
  121:   } else {
  122:     process.stdout.write('Config:     not found (using defaults)\n')
  123:   }
  124: 
  125:   return 0
  126: }
  127: 
  128: export function isHeartbeatRegistered(): boolean {
  129:   const sys = platform
  130:   if (sys === 'linux') return isCronRegistered()
  131:   if (sys === 'darwin') return isLaunchdRegistered()
  132:   if (sys === 'win32') return isSchtasksRegistered()
  133:   return false
  134: }
  135: 
  136: export function cmdRegisterProject(options: {
  137:   root: string
  138:   name?: string
  139:   worktree?: string
  140:   branch?: string
  141:   quickStart?: boolean
  142:   heartbeatInterval?: string
  143:   refreshInterval?: string
  144:   autoPush?: string
  145:   generateReport?: string
  146:   reportPort?: string
  147: }): number {
  148:   const root = resolve(options.root)
  149:   const config = loadConfig()
  150:   const existingProject = findProjectForPath(config, root)
  151: 
  152:   if (options.quickStart && options.branch !== undefined) {
  153:     process.stderr.write('Quick Start uses the default memorytree branch. Omit --branch to customize it.\n')
  154:     return 1
  155:   }
  156: 
  157:   const requestedBranch = options.quickStart
  158:     ? existingProject?.memory_branch ?? defaultProjectWorktreeBranch()
  159:     : options.branch ?? existingProject?.memory_branch ?? DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
  160:   if (!isValidWorktreeBranchName(requestedBranch)) {
  161:     process.stderr.write(`Invalid MemoryTree branch name: ${requestedBranch}\n`)
  162:     return 1
  163:   }
  164: 
  165:   const heartbeatInterval = options.quickStart
  166:     ? existingProject?.heartbeat_interval ?? QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
  167:     : options.heartbeatInterval ?? existingProject?.heartbeat_interval ?? config.heartbeat_interval
  168:   const refreshInterval = options.quickStart
  169:     ? existingProject?.refresh_interval ?? QUICK_START_REFRESH_INTERVAL
  170:     : options.refreshInterval ?? existingProject?.refresh_interval ?? QUICK_START_REFRESH_INTERVAL
  171:   const autoPush = options.quickStart
  172:     ? existingProject?.auto_push ?? true
  173:     : parseOptionalBoolean(options.autoPush) ?? existingProject?.auto_push ?? config.auto_push
  174:   const generateReport = options.quickStart
  175:     ? existingProject?.generate_report ?? true
  176:     : parseOptionalBoolean(options.generateReport) ?? existingProject?.generate_report ?? config.generate_report
  177: 
  178:   const requestedPort = options.reportPort ? parseInt(options.reportPort, 10) : NaN
  179:   const reportPort = Number.isInteger(requestedPort) && requestedPort > 0 && requestedPort <= 65535
  180:     ? requestedPort
  181:     : existingProject?.report_port ?? config.report_port
  182: 
  183:   const updated = upsertProject(config, root, {
  184:     name: options.name ?? existingProject?.name ?? basename(root),
  185:     development_path: root,
  186:     memory_path: options.worktree
  187:       ? resolve(options.worktree)
  188:       : existingProject?.memory_path ?? defaultProjectWorktreePath(root),
  189:     memory_branch: requestedBranch,
  190:     heartbeat_interval: heartbeatInterval,
  191:     refresh_interval: refreshInterval,
  192:     auto_push: autoPush,
  193:     generate_report: generateReport,
  194:     report_port: reportPort,
  195:   })
  196: 
  197:   const project = findProjectForPath(updated, root)
  198:   if (!project) {
  199:     process.stderr.write(`Failed to register project: ${root}\n`)
  200:     return 1
  201:   }
  202: 
  203:   const worktree = ensureProjectWorktree(project)
  204:   saveConfig(updated)
  205:   let upstream: ReturnType<typeof ensureBranchUpstream> | null = null
  206:   let upstreamError = ''
  207:   if (project.auto_push) {
  208:     try {
  209:       upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(project.memory_path, worktree.branch)
  210:     } catch (error: unknown) {
  211:       upstreamError = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
  212:     }
  213:   }
  214: 
  215:   process.stdout.write(`Registered project: ${project.name}\n`)
  216:   process.stdout.write(`Development path: ${project.development_path}\n`)
  217:   process.stdout.write(`Memory path: ${project.memory_path}\n`)
  218:   process.stdout.write(`Memory branch: ${project.memory_branch}\n`)
  219:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat interval: ${project.heartbeat_interval}\n`)
  220:   process.stdout.write(`Refresh interval: ${project.refresh_interval}\n`)
  221:   process.stdout.write(`Auto-push: ${project.auto_push}\n`)
  222:   process.stdout.write(`Generate report: ${project.generate_report}\n`)
  223:   process.stdout.write(`Worktree branch: ${worktree.branch}\n`)
  224:   process.stdout.write(`Worktree created: ${worktree.created ? 'yes' : 'no'}\n`)
  225:   if (upstreamError) {
  226:     process.stderr.write(`Upstream configured: failed (${upstreamError})\n`)
  227:   } else if (upstream === null) {
  228:     process.stdout.write('Upstream configured: skipped (auto_push disabled)\n')
  229:   } else if (upstream.remote === null) {
  230:     process.stdout.write('Upstream configured: no remote available\n')
  231:   } else {
  232:     process.stdout.write(`Upstream configured: ${upstream.created ? 'yes' : 'already'} (${upstream.remote}/${worktree.branch})\n`)
  233:   }
  234:   return upstreamError ? 1 : 0
  235: }
  236: 
  237: export async function cmdQuickStart(options: {
  238:   root: string
  239:   name?: string
  240: }): Promise<number> {
  241:   const root = resolve(options.root)
  242:   const config = loadConfig()
  243:   const quickStartSeconds = intervalToSeconds(QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
  244:   const schedulerIntervalSeconds = registeredHeartbeatIntervalSeconds()
  245:   const effectiveSchedulerSeconds = schedulerIntervalSeconds ?? intervalToSeconds(config.heartbeat_interval)
  246:   const effectiveSchedulerLabel = schedulerIntervalSeconds === null
  247:     ? config.heartbeat_interval
  248:     : formatIntervalSeconds(schedulerIntervalSeconds)
  249: 
  250:   process.stdout.write('Quick Start: prepare scheduler, register this repository, and run one sync now.\n')
  251: 
  252:   if (!isHeartbeatRegistered()) {
  253:     process.stdout.write('Step 1/3: installing heartbeat scheduler with recommended defaults.\n')
  254:     const installResult = cmdInstall({ interval: QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL, autoPush: 'true' })
  255:     if (installResult !== 0) {
  256:       return installResult
  257:     }
  258:   } else if (effectiveSchedulerSeconds > quickStartSeconds) {
  259:     process.stdout.write(
  260:       `Step 1/3: heartbeat scheduler is registered at ${effectiveSchedulerLabel}; ` +
  261:       `reinstalling it to ${QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL} so Quick Start can run on time.\n`,
  262:     )
  263:     const uninstallResult = cmdUninstall()
  264:     if (uninstallResult !== 0) {
  265:       return uninstallResult
  266:     }
  267: 
  268:     const installResult = cmdInstall({
  269:       interval: QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
  270:       autoPush: config.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false',
  271:     })
  272:     if (installResult !== 0) {
  273:       return installResult
  274:     }
  275:   } else {
  276:     process.stdout.write(
  277:       `Step 1/3: heartbeat scheduler already registered at ${effectiveSchedulerLabel}; keeping the existing install.\n`,
  278:     )
  279:   }
  280: 
  281:   process.stdout.write('Step 2/3: registering the current repository with Quick Start settings.\n')
  282:   const registerOptions: Parameters<typeof cmdRegisterProject>[0] = {
  283:     root,
  284:     quickStart: true,
  285:   }
  286:   if (options.name !== undefined) {
  287:     registerOptions.name = options.name
  288:   }
  289: 
  290:   const registerResult = cmdRegisterProject(registerOptions)
  291:   if (registerResult !== 0) {
  292:     return registerResult
  293:   }
  294: 
  295:   process.stdout.write('Step 3/3: running one immediate heartbeat sync.\n')
  296:   return await cmdRunOnce({
  297:     root,
  298:     force: true,
  299:   })
  300: }
  301: 
  302: export function registeredHeartbeatIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  303:   const sys = platform
  304:   if (sys === 'linux') return cronRegisteredIntervalSeconds()
  305:   if (sys === 'darwin') return launchdRegisteredIntervalSeconds()
  306:   if (sys === 'win32') return schtasksRegisteredIntervalSeconds()
  307:   return null
  308: }
  309: 
  310: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  311: // Linux (cron)
  312: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  313: 
  314: function installCron(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {
  315:   if (isCronRegistered()) {
  316:     process.stderr.write("Heartbeat is already registered in cron. Use 'uninstall' first.\n")
  317:     return 1
  318:   }
  319: 
  320:   const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.floor(seconds / 60))
  321:   const logDir = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs')
  322:   mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true })
  323: 
  324:   const cronLine = `*/${minutes} * * * * node "${scriptPath}" daemon run-once >> "${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-cron.log')}" 2>&1 # memorytree`
  325:   const existing = getCrontab()
  326:   const newCrontab = existing.trim()
  327:     ? existing.trimEnd() + '\n' + cronLine + '\n'
  328:     : cronLine + '\n'
  329: 
  330:   try {
  331:     const tmpFile = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', '.crontab.tmp')
  332:     writeFileSync(tmpFile, newCrontab)
  333:     execCommand('crontab', [tmpFile])
  334:     unlinkSync(tmpFile)
  335:   } catch {
  336:     process.stderr.write('Failed to install cron job.\n')
  337:     return 1
  338:   }
  339: 
  340:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered in cron (every ${minutes}m).\n`)
  341:   return 0
  342: }
  343: 
  344: function uninstallCron(): number {
  345:   const existing = getCrontab()
  346:   const filtered = existing.split('\n').filter(line => !line.includes('memorytree')).join('\n') + '\n'
  347:   try {
  348:     const tmpFile = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', '.crontab.tmp')
  349:     mkdirSync(dirname(tmpFile), { recursive: true })
  350:     writeFileSync(tmpFile, filtered)
  351:     execCommand('crontab', [tmpFile])
  352:     unlinkSync(tmpFile)
  353:   } catch {
  354:     // best effort
  355:   }
  356:   process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from cron.\n')
  357:   return 0
  358: }
  359: 
  360: export function isCronRegistered(): boolean {
  361:   return getCrontab().includes('memorytree')
  362: }
  363: 
  364: function getCrontab(): string {
  365:   try {
  366:     return execCommand('crontab', ['-l'], { allowFailure: true })
  367:   } catch {
  368:     return ''
  369:   }
  370: }
  371: 
  372: function cronRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  373:   const line = getCrontab()
  374:     .split(/\r?\n/)
  375:     .find(candidate => candidate.includes('memorytree'))
  376:   if (!line) return null
  377: 
  378:   const match = line.match(/^\*\/(\d+)\s+\*\s+\*\s+\*\s+\*.*#\s*memorytree\s*$/)
  379:   if (!match) return null
  380: 
  381:   const minutes = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
  382:   return Number.isInteger(minutes) && minutes > 0 ? minutes * 60 : null
  383: }
  384: 
  385: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  386: // macOS (launchd)
  387: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  388: 
  389: function launchdPlistPath(): string {
  390:   return resolve(homedir(), 'Library', 'LaunchAgents', `${LAUNCHD_LABEL}.plist`)
  391: }
  392: 
  393: function installLaunchd(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {
  394:   const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()
  395:   if (existsSync(plistPath)) {
  396:     process.stderr.write("Heartbeat plist already exists. Use 'uninstall' first.\n")
  397:     return 1
  398:   }
  399: 
  400:   const logDir = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs')
  401:   mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true })
  402: 
  403:   const plistContent = `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
  404: <!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
  405: <plist version="1.0">
  406: <dict>
  407:     <key>Label</key>
  408:     <string>${LAUNCHD_LABEL}</string>
  409:     <key>ProgramArguments</key>
  410:     <array>
  411:         <string>node</string>
  412:         <string>${scriptPath}</string>
  413:         <string>daemon</string>
  414:         <string>run-once</string>
  415:     </array>
  416:     <key>StartInterval</key>
  417:     <integer>${seconds}</integer>
  418:     <key>StandardOutPath</key>
  419:     <string>${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-launchd.log')}</string>
  420:     <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
  421:     <string>${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-launchd.log')}</string>
  422: </dict>
  423: </plist>
  424: `
  425:   mkdirSync(dirname(plistPath), { recursive: true })
  426:   writeFileSync(plistPath, plistContent, 'utf-8')
  427: 
  428:   try {
  429:     execCommand('launchctl', ['load', plistPath])
  430:   } catch {
  431:     process.stderr.write('Failed to load plist.\n')
  432:     return 1
  433:   }
  434: 
  435:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered via launchd (every ${seconds}s).\n`)
  436:   return 0
  437: }
  438: 
  439: function uninstallLaunchd(): number {
  440:   const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()
  441:   if (existsSync(plistPath)) {
  442:     try { execCommand('launchctl', ['unload', plistPath], { allowFailure: true }) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  443:     try { unlinkSync(plistPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  444:   }
  445:   process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from launchd.\n')
  446:   return 0
  447: }
  448: 
  449: export function isLaunchdRegistered(): boolean {
  450:   return existsSync(launchdPlistPath())
  451: }
  452: 
  453: function launchdRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  454:   const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()
  455:   if (!existsSync(plistPath)) return null
  456: 
  457:   try {
  458:     const text = readFileSync(plistPath, 'utf-8')
  459:     const match = text.match(/<key>StartInterval<\/key>\s*<integer>(\d+)<\/integer>/i)
  460:     if (!match) return null
  461: 
  462:     const seconds = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
  463:     return Number.isInteger(seconds) && seconds > 0 ? seconds : null
  464:   } catch {
  465:     return null
  466:   }
  467: }
  468: 
  469: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  470: // Windows (Task Scheduler)
  471: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  472: 
  473: // Write a VBScript launcher that runs node silently (no console window).
  474: // wscript.exe /B runs in batch mode (suppresses script error popups).
  475: // VBScript Run(..., 0, False): 0 = SW_HIDE hides the window; False = fire-and-forget.
  476: // Written as UTF-16 LE with BOM so wscript.exe handles non-ASCII paths (e.g. Chinese
  477: // usernames) correctly on all Windows versions.
  478: export function vbsLauncherPath(): string {
  479:   return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'heartbeat-launcher.vbs')
  480: }
  481: 
  482: export function writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath: string): string {
  483:   const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()
  484:   mkdirSync(dirname(vbsPath), { recursive: true })
  485:   // Inside VBScript strings, double-quote is escaped as ""
  486:   const escapedScript = scriptPath.replace(/"/g, '""')
  487:   // Use the full node binary path so the launcher works even when Task Scheduler's
  488:   // PATH differs from the user's shell (e.g. nvm/fnm environments).
  489:   const escapedNode = process.execPath.replace(/"/g, '""')
  490:   const content = [
  491:     'Set WshShell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")',
  492:     `WshShell.Run """${escapedNode}"" ""${escapedScript}"" daemon run-once", 0, False`,
  493:   ].join('\r\n') + '\r\n'
  494:   // UTF-16 LE BOM: recognised by wscript.exe on all Windows versions and preserves
  495:   // non-ASCII characters (e.g. Chinese home directory paths) without corruption.
  496:   const buf = Buffer.concat([Buffer.from([0xff, 0xfe]), Buffer.from(content, 'utf16le')])
  497:   writeFileSync(vbsPath, buf)
  498:   return vbsPath
  499: }
  500: 
  501: function installSchtasks(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {
  502:   if (isSchtasksRegistered()) {
  503:     process.stderr.write("Heartbeat is already registered in Task Scheduler. Use 'uninstall' first.\n")
  504:     return 1
  505:   }
  506: 
  507:   const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.floor(seconds / 60))
  508:   const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath)
  509:   const trCommand = `wscript.exe /B "${vbsPath}"`
  510: 
  511:   try {
  512:     execCommand('schtasks', [
  513:       '/create', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/sc', 'minute', '/mo', String(minutes),
  514:       '/tr', trCommand, '/f',
  515:     ])
  516:   } catch {
  517:     // schtasks failed 鈥?remove the launcher file we just wrote to keep state clean
  518:     try { unlinkSync(vbsPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  519:     process.stderr.write('Failed to create scheduled task.\n')
  520:     return 1
  521:   }
  522: 
  523:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered in Task Scheduler (every ${minutes}m).\n`)
  524:   return 0
  525: }
  526: 
  527: function uninstallSchtasks(): number {
  528:   try {
  529:     execCommand('schtasks', ['/delete', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/f'], { allowFailure: true })
  530:   } catch {
  531:     // best effort
  532:   }
  533:   const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()
  534:   if (existsSync(vbsPath)) {
  535:     try { unlinkSync(vbsPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  536:   }
  537:   process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from Task Scheduler.\n')
  538:   return 0
  539: }
  540: 
  541: export function isSchtasksRegistered(): boolean {
  542:   try {
  543:     execCommand('schtasks', ['/query', '/tn', TASK_NAME])
  544:     return true
  545:   } catch {
  546:     return false
  547:   }
  548: }
  549: 
  550: function schtasksRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  551:   try {
  552:     const xml = execCommand('schtasks', ['/query', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/xml'], { allowFailure: true })
  553:     if (!xml.trim()) return null
  554: 
  555:     const match = xml.match(/<Interval>([^<]+)<\/Interval>/i)
  556:     if (!match) return null
  557: 
  558:     return parseIsoDurationToSeconds(match[1]!)
  559:   } catch {
  560:     return null
  561:   }
  562: }
  563: 
  564: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  565: // Helpers
  566: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  567: 
  568: export function heartbeatScriptPath(): string {
  569:   // Use process.argv[1] which is the actual script being executed,
  570:   // more reliable than import.meta.url which varies between tsx and bundled output
  571:   const scriptArg = process.argv[1] ?? ''
  572:   if (scriptArg && scriptArg.endsWith('cli.js')) {
  573:     return resolve(scriptArg)
  574:   }
  575:   return fallbackHeartbeatScriptPath(import.meta.url)
  576: }
  577: 
  578: export function fallbackHeartbeatScriptPath(moduleUrl: string): string {
  579:   const moduleDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(moduleUrl))
  580:   if (basename(moduleDir).toLowerCase() === 'dist') {
  581:     return resolve(moduleDir, 'cli.js')
  582:   }
  583:   return resolve(moduleDir, '..', '..', 'dist', 'cli.js')
  584: }
  585: 
  586: function parseOptionalBoolean(value: string | undefined): boolean | undefined {
  587:   if (value === undefined) return undefined
  588:   if (value === 'true') return true
  589:   if (value === 'false') return false
  590:   return undefined
  591: }
  592: 
  593: function parseIsoDurationToSeconds(value: string): number | null {
  594:   const match = value.trim().match(/^P(?:(\d+)D)?(?:T(?:(\d+)H)?(?:(\d+)M)?(?:(\d+)S)?)?$/i)
  595:   if (!match) return null
  596: 
  597:   const days = parseInt(match[1] ?? '0', 10)
  598:   const hours = parseInt(match[2] ?? '0', 10)
  599:   const minutes = parseInt(match[3] ?? '0', 10)
  600:   const seconds = parseInt(match[4] ?? '0', 10)
  601:   const total = (days * 86400) + (hours * 3600) + (minutes * 60) + seconds
  602:   return total > 0 ? total : null
  603: }

  605: function formatIntervalSeconds(seconds: number): string {
  606:   if (seconds % 3600 === 0) return `${String(seconds / 3600)}h`
  607:   if (seconds % 60 === 0) return `${String(seconds / 60)}m`
  608:   return `${String(seconds)}s`
  609: }

  254:     const installResult = cmdInstall({ interval: QUICK_START_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL, autoPush: 'true' })

  278:     )

  282:   const registerOptions: Parameters<typeof cmdRegisterProject>[0] = {

  302: export function registeredHeartbeatIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  303:   const sys = platform
  304:   if (sys === 'linux') return cronRegisteredIntervalSeconds()
  305:   if (sys === 'darwin') return launchdRegisteredIntervalSeconds()
  306:   if (sys === 'win32') return schtasksRegisteredIntervalSeconds()

  310: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  311: // Linux (cron)
  312: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  313: 
  314: function installCron(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {
  315:   if (isCronRegistered()) {
  316:     process.stderr.write("Heartbeat is already registered in cron. Use 'uninstall' first.\n")
  317:     return 1
  318:   }
  319: 
  320:   const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.floor(seconds / 60))
  321:   const logDir = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs')
  322:   mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true })
  323: 
  324:   const cronLine = `*/${minutes} * * * * node "${scriptPath}" daemon run-once >> "${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-cron.log')}" 2>&1 # memorytree`
  325:   const existing = getCrontab()
  326:   const newCrontab = existing.trim()
  327:     ? existing.trimEnd() + '\n' + cronLine + '\n'
  328:     : cronLine + '\n'
  329: 
  330:   try {
  331:     const tmpFile = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', '.crontab.tmp')
  332:     writeFileSync(tmpFile, newCrontab)
  333:     execCommand('crontab', [tmpFile])
  334:     unlinkSync(tmpFile)
  335:   } catch {
  336:     process.stderr.write('Failed to install cron job.\n')
  337:     return 1
  338:   }
  339: 
  340:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered in cron (every ${minutes}m).\n`)
  341:   return 0
  342: }
  343: 
  344: function uninstallCron(): number {
  345:   const existing = getCrontab()
  346:   const filtered = existing.split('\n').filter(line => !line.includes('memorytree')).join('\n') + '\n'
  347:   try {
  348:     const tmpFile = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', '.crontab.tmp')
  349:     mkdirSync(dirname(tmpFile), { recursive: true })
  350:     writeFileSync(tmpFile, filtered)
  351:     execCommand('crontab', [tmpFile])
  352:     unlinkSync(tmpFile)
  353:   } catch {
  354:     // best effort
  355:   }
  356:   process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from cron.\n')
  357:   return 0
  358: }
  359: 
  360: export function isCronRegistered(): boolean {
  361:   return getCrontab().includes('memorytree')
  362: }
  363: 
  364: function getCrontab(): string {
  365:   try {
  366:     return execCommand('crontab', ['-l'], { allowFailure: true })
  367:   } catch {
  368:     return ''
  369:   }
  370: }
  371: 
  372: function cronRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  373:   const line = getCrontab()
  374:     .split(/\r?\n/)
  375:     .find(candidate => candidate.includes('memorytree'))
  376:   if (!line) return null
  377: 
  378:   const match = line.match(/^\*\/(\d+)\s+\*\s+\*\s+\*\s+\*.*#\s*memorytree\s*$/)
  379:   if (!match) return null
  380: 
  381:   const minutes = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
  382:   return Number.isInteger(minutes) && minutes > 0 ? minutes * 60 : null
  383: }

  385: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  386: // macOS (launchd)
  387: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  388: 
  389: function launchdPlistPath(): string {
  390:   return resolve(homedir(), 'Library', 'LaunchAgents', `${LAUNCHD_LABEL}.plist`)
  391: }
  392: 
  393: function installLaunchd(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {
  394:   const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()
  395:   if (existsSync(plistPath)) {
  396:     process.stderr.write("Heartbeat plist already exists. Use 'uninstall' first.\n")
  397:     return 1
  398:   }
  399: 
  400:   const logDir = resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'logs')
  401:   mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true })
  402: 
  403:   const plistContent = `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
  404: <!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
  405: <plist version="1.0">
  406: <dict>
  407:     <key>Label</key>
  408:     <string>${LAUNCHD_LABEL}</string>
  409:     <key>ProgramArguments</key>
  410:     <array>
  411:         <string>node</string>
  412:         <string>${scriptPath}</string>
  413:         <string>daemon</string>
  414:         <string>run-once</string>
  415:     </array>
  416:     <key>StartInterval</key>
  417:     <integer>${seconds}</integer>
  418:     <key>StandardOutPath</key>
  419:     <string>${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-launchd.log')}</string>
  420:     <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
  421:     <string>${resolve(logDir, 'heartbeat-launchd.log')}</string>
  422: </dict>
  423: </plist>
  424: `
  425:   mkdirSync(dirname(plistPath), { recursive: true })
  426:   writeFileSync(plistPath, plistContent, 'utf-8')
  427: 
  428:   try {
  429:     execCommand('launchctl', ['load', plistPath])
  430:   } catch {
  431:     process.stderr.write('Failed to load plist.\n')
  432:     return 1
  433:   }
  434: 
  435:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered via launchd (every ${seconds}s).\n`)
  436:   return 0
  437: }
  438: 
  439: function uninstallLaunchd(): number {
  440:   const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()
  441:   if (existsSync(plistPath)) {
  442:     try { execCommand('launchctl', ['unload', plistPath], { allowFailure: true }) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  443:     try { unlinkSync(plistPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  444:   }
  445:   process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from launchd.\n')
  446:   return 0
  447: }
  448: 
  449: export function isLaunchdRegistered(): boolean {
  450:   return existsSync(launchdPlistPath())
  451: }
  452: 
  453: function launchdRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  454:   const plistPath = launchdPlistPath()
  455:   if (!existsSync(plistPath)) return null
  456: 
  457:   try {
  458:     const text = readFileSync(plistPath, 'utf-8')
  459:     const match = text.match(/<key>StartInterval<\/key>\s*<integer>(\d+)<\/integer>/i)
  460:     if (!match) return null
  461: 
  462:     const seconds = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
  463:     return Number.isInteger(seconds) && seconds > 0 ? seconds : null
  464:   } catch {
  465:     return null
  466:   }
  467: }

  469: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  470: // Windows (Task Scheduler)
  471: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  472: 
  473: // Write a VBScript launcher that runs node silently (no console window).
  474: // wscript.exe /B runs in batch mode (suppresses script error popups).
  475: // VBScript Run(..., 0, False): 0 = SW_HIDE hides the window; False = fire-and-forget.
  476: // Written as UTF-16 LE with BOM so wscript.exe handles non-ASCII paths (e.g. Chinese
  477: // usernames) correctly on all Windows versions.
  478: export function vbsLauncherPath(): string {
  479:   return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'heartbeat-launcher.vbs')
  480: }
  481: 
  482: export function writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath: string): string {
  483:   const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()
  484:   mkdirSync(dirname(vbsPath), { recursive: true })
  485:   // Inside VBScript strings, double-quote is escaped as ""
  486:   const escapedScript = scriptPath.replace(/"/g, '""')
  487:   // Use the full node binary path so the launcher works even when Task Scheduler's
  488:   // PATH differs from the user's shell (e.g. nvm/fnm environments).
  489:   const escapedNode = process.execPath.replace(/"/g, '""')
  490:   const content = [
  491:     'Set WshShell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")',
  492:     `WshShell.Run """${escapedNode}"" ""${escapedScript}"" daemon run-once", 0, False`,
  493:   ].join('\r\n') + '\r\n'
  494:   // UTF-16 LE BOM: recognised by wscript.exe on all Windows versions and preserves
  495:   // non-ASCII characters (e.g. Chinese home directory paths) without corruption.
  496:   const buf = Buffer.concat([Buffer.from([0xff, 0xfe]), Buffer.from(content, 'utf16le')])
  497:   writeFileSync(vbsPath, buf)
  498:   return vbsPath
  499: }
  500: 
  501: function installSchtasks(scriptPath: string, seconds: number): number {
  502:   if (isSchtasksRegistered()) {
  503:     process.stderr.write("Heartbeat is already registered in Task Scheduler. Use 'uninstall' first.\n")
  504:     return 1
  505:   }
  506: 
  507:   const minutes = Math.max(1, Math.floor(seconds / 60))
  508:   const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath)
  509:   const trCommand = `wscript.exe /B "${vbsPath}"`
  510: 
  511:   try {
  512:     execCommand('schtasks', [
  513:       '/create', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/sc', 'minute', '/mo', String(minutes),
  514:       '/tr', trCommand, '/f',
  515:     ])
  516:   } catch {
  517:     // schtasks failed 鈥?remove the launcher file we just wrote to keep state clean
  518:     try { unlinkSync(vbsPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  519:     process.stderr.write('Failed to create scheduled task.\n')
  520:     return 1
  521:   }
  522: 
  523:   process.stdout.write(`Heartbeat registered in Task Scheduler (every ${minutes}m).\n`)
  524:   return 0
  525: }
  526: 
  527: function uninstallSchtasks(): number {
  528:   try {
  529:     execCommand('schtasks', ['/delete', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/f'], { allowFailure: true })
  530:   } catch {
  531:     // best effort
  532:   }
  533:   const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()
  534:   if (existsSync(vbsPath)) {
  535:     try { unlinkSync(vbsPath) } catch { /* ignore */ }
  536:   }
  537:   process.stdout.write('Heartbeat removed from Task Scheduler.\n')
  538:   return 0
  539: }
  540: 
  541: export function isSchtasksRegistered(): boolean {
  542:   try {
  543:     execCommand('schtasks', ['/query', '/tn', TASK_NAME])
  544:     return true
  545:   } catch {
  546:     return false
  547:   }
  548: }
  549: 
  550: function schtasksRegisteredIntervalSeconds(): number | null {
  551:   try {
  552:     const xml = execCommand('schtasks', ['/query', '/tn', TASK_NAME, '/xml'], { allowFailure: true })
  553:     if (!xml.trim()) return null
  554: 
  555:     const match = xml.match(/<Interval>([^<]+)<\/Interval>/i)
  556:     if (!match) return null
  557: 
  558:     return parseIsoDurationToSeconds(match[1]!)
  559:   } catch {
  560:     return null
  561:   }
  562: }
  563: 
  564: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  565: // Helpers
  566: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  567: 
  568: export function heartbeatScriptPath(): string {
  569:   // Use process.argv[1] which is the actual script being executed,
  570:   // more reliable than import.meta.url which varies between tsx and bundled output
  571:   const scriptArg = process.argv[1] ?? ''
  572:   if (scriptArg && scriptArg.endsWith('cli.js')) {
  573:     return resolve(scriptArg)
  574:   }
  575:   return fallbackHeartbeatScriptPath(import.meta.url)
  576: }
  577: 
  578: export function fallbackHeartbeatScriptPath(moduleUrl: string): string {
  579:   const moduleDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(moduleUrl))
  580:   if (basename(moduleDir).toLowerCase() === 'dist') {
  581:     return resolve(moduleDir, 'cli.js')
  582:   }
  583:   return resolve(moduleDir, '..', '..', 'dist', 'cli.js')
  584: }
  585: 
  586: function parseOptionalBoolean(value: string | undefined): boolean | undefined {
  587:   if (value === undefined) return undefined
  588:   if (value === 'true') return true
  589:   if (value === 'false') return false
  590:   return undefined
  591: }
  592: 
  593: function parseIsoDurationToSeconds(value: string): number | null {
  594:   const match = value.trim().match(/^P(?:(\d+)D)?(?:T(?:(\d+)H)?(?:(\d+)M)?(?:(\d+)S)?)?$/i)
  595:   if (!match) return null
  596: 
  597:   const days = parseInt(match[1] ?? '0', 10)
  598:   const hours = parseInt(match[2] ?? '0', 10)
  599:   const minutes = parseInt(match[3] ?? '0', 10)
  600:   const seconds = parseInt(match[4] ?? '0', 10)
  601:   const total = (days * 86400) + (hours * 3600) + (minutes * 60) + seconds
  602:   return total > 0 ? total : null
  603: }
  604: 
  605: function formatIntervalSeconds(seconds: number): string {
  606:   if (seconds % 3600 === 0) return `${String(seconds / 3600)}h`
  607:   if (seconds % 60 === 0) return `${String(seconds / 60)}m`
  608:   return `${String(seconds)}s`
  609: }

    1: /**
    2:  * CLI: memorytree init 鈥?initialize a MemoryTree workspace.
    3:  * Port of scripts/init-memorytree.py
    4:  */
    5: 
    6: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    7: import { resolve, join } from 'node:path'
    8: 
    9: import {
   10:   buildDatetime,
   11:   createMemoryDirs,
   12:   findExternalPolicySources,
   13:   resolveScaffoldPaths,
   14:   resolveTemplateDir,
   15:   scaffoldContentFiles,
   16:   writeTemplate,
   17: } from '../project/scaffold.js'
   18: import { resolveSkillRoot } from '../utils/path.js'
   19: 
   20: export interface InitOptions {
   21:   root: string
   22:   projectName: string
   23:   goalSummary: string
   24:   locale: string
   25:   date: string
   26:   time: string
   27:   skipAgents: boolean
   28:   force: boolean
   29: }
   30: 
   31: export function cmdInit(options: InitOptions): number {
   32:   const root = resolve(options.root)
   33:   if (!existsSync(root)) {
   34:     process.stderr.write(`root does not exist: ${root}\n`)
   35:     return 1
   36:   }
   37: 
   38:   if (options.skipAgents) {
   39:     process.stderr.write(
   40:       '--skip-agents is not supported for fresh initialization. ' +
   41:       'Use memorytree upgrade when preserving or merging an existing AGENTS.md.\n',
   42:     )
   43:     return 1
   44:   }
   45: 
   46:   const agentsPath = join(root, 'AGENTS.md')
   47:   if (existsSync(agentsPath)) {
   48:     process.stderr.write(
   49:       'AGENTS.md already exists. Use memorytree upgrade to preserve or merge existing repo policy.\n',
   50:     )
   51:     return 1
   52:   }
   53: 
   54:   const policySources = findExternalPolicySources(root)
   55:   if (policySources.length > 0) {
   56:     const preview = policySources.slice(0, 3).join(', ') + (policySources.length > 3 ? ', ...' : '')
   57:     process.stderr.write(
   58:       `External repo policy files detected (${preview}). ` +
   59:       'Use memorytree upgrade to preserve host commit and PR rules.\n',
   60:     )
   61:     return 1
   62:   }
   63: 
   64:   const skillRoot = resolveSkillRoot(import.meta.url)
   65:   const templates = resolveTemplateDir(skillRoot, root, options.locale)
   66:   const dt = buildDatetime(options.date, options.time)
   67: 
   68:   createMemoryDirs(root)
   69:   const paths = resolveScaffoldPaths(root, dt)
   70:   scaffoldContentFiles(paths, templates, options.goalSummary, options.projectName, options.force)
   71:   writeTemplate(join(templates, 'agents.md'), agentsPath, options.force, {})
   72:   writeHeartbeatNextStep(root)
   73: 
   74:   return 0
   75: }
   76: 
   77: function writeHeartbeatNextStep(root: string): void {
   78:   const displayRoot = root.includes(' ') ? `"${root}"` : root
   79:   process.stdout.write(`Initialized MemoryTree files in: ${root}\n`)
   80:   process.stdout.write('This command did not register heartbeat or modify ~/.memorytree/config.toml.\n')
   81:   process.stdout.write('If you want the default heartbeat setup for this repository, run:\n')
   82:   process.stdout.write(`  memorytree daemon quick-start --root ${displayRoot}\n`)
   83: }

    7: import { resolve } from 'node:path'

   81: export function buildDatetime(dateValue: string, timeValue: string): Date {

   84:   }
   85:   if (dateValue) {
   86:     return new Date(`${dateValue}T00:00:00`)
   87:   }
   88:   if (timeValue) {
   89:     const today = new Date()
   90:     const y = today.getFullYear()
   91:     const m = String(today.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')
   92:     const d = String(today.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')
   93:     return new Date(`${y}-${m}-${d}T${timeValue}:00`)
   94:   }
   95:   return new Date()
   96: }

   98: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  101: 
  102: export function resolveTemplateDir(skillRoot: string, root: string, localeValue: string): string {

  106:   if (!existsSync(templateDir)) {

  108:   }
  109:   return templateDir

  115: 
  116: export function findExternalPolicySources(root: string): string[] {

  122:       matches.add(toPosixPath(relative(root, p)))

  126:   for (const pattern of CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS) {
  127:     const p = join(root, pattern)
  128:     if (existsSync(p) && isFile(p) && hasPolicyContent(p)) {
  129:       matches.add(toPosixPath(relative(root, p)))
  130:     }
  131:   }
  132: 
  133:   return [...matches].sort()
  134: }

  142:   }

  152:   const versions: string[] = []

  158:       }
  159:     }
  160:   } catch {
  161:     return null
  162:   }
  163:   return versions.length > 0 ? versions.sort().pop()! : null
  164: }

  173:       if (match) {

  175:       }

  179:   }
  180:   if (matches.length === 0) return null
  181:   matches.sort()

  234: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  235: // Template writing
  236: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  237: 
  238: export function writeTemplate(
  239:   templatePath: string,
  240:   targetPath: string,
  241:   force: boolean,
  242:   values: Record<string, string>,
  243: ): boolean {
  244:   if (existsSync(targetPath) && !force) return false
  245:   let content = readFileSync(templatePath, 'utf-8')
  246:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(values)) {
  247:     content = content.replaceAll(`{{${key}}}`, value)
  248:   }

  250:   writeFileSync(targetPath, content, 'utf-8')
  251:   return true
  252: }

  258: export function resolveScaffoldPaths(root: string, dt: Date): ScaffoldPaths {
  259:   const y = dt.getFullYear()
  260:   const m = String(dt.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')
  261:   const d = String(dt.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')
  262:   const hh = String(dt.getHours()).padStart(2, '0')
  263:   const mm = String(dt.getMinutes()).padStart(2, '0')
  264: 
  265:   const dateToken = `${y}${m}${d}`
  266:   const dateLabel = `${y}-${m}-${d}`
  267:   const timeLabel = `${hh}:${mm}`
  268:   const chatFilename = `${y}-${m}-${d}_${hh}-${mm}.md`
  269: 
  270:   const goalDir = join(root, 'Memory', '01_goals')
  271:   const todoDir = join(root, 'Memory', '02_todos')
  272:   const chatDir = join(root, 'Memory', '03_chat_logs')
  273: 
  274:   let goalVersion = findLatestVersion(goalDir, /^goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md$/)
  275:   if (goalVersion === null) goalVersion = '001'
  276:   const prevNum = parseInt(goalVersion, 10)
  277:   const previousVersion = prevNum > 1 ? `v${String(prevNum - 1).padStart(3, '0')}` : 'none'
  278: 
  279:   let todoVersion = findLatestTodoVersion(todoDir, goalVersion)
  280:   if (todoVersion === null) todoVersion = '001'
  281: 
  282:   const goalPath = findLatestGoalFile(goalDir) ?? join(goalDir, `goal_v${goalVersion}_${dateToken}.md`)
  283:   const todoPath = findLatestTodoFile(todoDir, goalVersion) ?? join(todoDir, `todo_v${goalVersion}_${todoVersion}_${dateToken}.md`)
  284:   const chatPath = findLatestChatLogFile(chatDir) ?? join(chatDir, chatFilename)
  285: 
  286:   return {
  287:     root,
  288:     goalDir,
  289:     todoDir,
  290:     chatDir,
  291:     goalPath,
  292:     todoPath,
  293:     chatPath,
  294:     goalVersion,
  295:     todoVersion,
  296:     previousVersion,
  297:     dateLabel,
  298:     timeLabel,
  299:   }
  300: }
  301:

  303: // Directory creation
  304: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  306: export function createMemoryDirs(root: string): string[] {
  307:   const created: string[] = []
  308:   for (const rel of MEMORY_DIRS) {
  309:     const p = join(root, rel)
  310:     if (!existsSync(p)) {
  311:       created.push(toPosixPath(rel))
  312:     }
  313:     mkdirSync(p, { recursive: true })
  314:   }
  315:   return created
  316: }

  319: // Content scaffolding
  320: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  321: 
  322: export function scaffoldContentFiles(
  323:   paths: ScaffoldPaths,
  324:   templates: string,
  325:   goalSummary: string,
  326:   projectName: string,
  327:   force: boolean,
  328: ): { created: string[]; preserved: string[] } {
  329:   const created: string[] = []
  330:   const preserved: string[] = []
  331: 
  332:   const pairs: Array<[string, string, Record<string, string>]> = [
  333:     [
  334:       join(templates, 'goal.md'),
  335:       paths.goalPath,
  336:       {
  337:         GOAL_VERSION: paths.goalVersion,
  338:         DATE: paths.dateLabel,
  339:         PREVIOUS_VERSION: paths.previousVersion,
  340:         GOAL_SUMMARY: goalSummary.trim(),
  341:       },
  342:     ],
  343:     [
  344:       join(templates, 'todo.md'),
  345:       paths.todoPath,
  346:       {
  347:         GOAL_VERSION: paths.goalVersion,
  348:         TODO_SUBVERSION: paths.todoVersion,
  349:         DATE: paths.dateLabel,
  350:       },
  351:     ],
  352:     [
  353:       join(templates, 'chat-log.md'),
  354:       paths.chatPath,
  355:       {
  356:         DATE: paths.dateLabel,
  357:         TIME: paths.timeLabel,
  358:         PROJECT_NAME: projectName.trim(),
  359:       },
  360:     ],
  361:   ]
  362: 
  363:   for (const [templatePath, targetPath, values] of pairs) {
  364:     if (writeTemplate(templatePath, targetPath, force, values)) {
  365:       created.push(toPosixPath(relative(paths.root, targetPath)))
  366:     } else {
  367:       preserved.push(toPosixPath(relative(paths.root, targetPath)))
  368:     }
  369:   }
  370: 
  371:   return { created, preserved }
  372: }

  379:   try { return statSync(p).isFile() } catch { return false }
  380: }

  274:   let goalVersion = findLatestVersion(goalDir, /^goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md$/)
  275:   if (goalVersion === null) goalVersion = '001'
  276:   const prevNum = parseInt(goalVersion, 10)
  277:   const previousVersion = prevNum > 1 ? `v${String(prevNum - 1).padStart(3, '0')}` : 'none'
  278: 
  279:   let todoVersion = findLatestTodoVersion(todoDir, goalVersion)
  280:   if (todoVersion === null) todoVersion = '001'
  281: 
  282:   const goalPath = findLatestGoalFile(goalDir) ?? join(goalDir, `goal_v${goalVersion}_${dateToken}.md`)
  283:   const todoPath = findLatestTodoFile(todoDir, goalVersion) ?? join(todoDir, `todo_v${goalVersion}_${todoVersion}_${dateToken}.md`)
  284:   const chatPath = findLatestChatLogFile(chatDir) ?? join(chatDir, chatFilename)
  285: 
  286:   return {
  287:     root,
  288:     goalDir,
  289:     todoDir,
  290:     chatDir,
  291:     goalPath,
  292:     todoPath,
  293:     chatPath,
  294:     goalVersion,
  295:     todoVersion,
  296:     previousVersion,
  297:     dateLabel,
  298:     timeLabel,
  299:   }
  300: }
  301: 
  302: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  303: // Directory creation
  304: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  305: 
  306: export function createMemoryDirs(root: string): string[] {
  307:   const created: string[] = []
  308:   for (const rel of MEMORY_DIRS) {
  309:     const p = join(root, rel)
  310:     if (!existsSync(p)) {
  311:       created.push(toPosixPath(rel))
  312:     }
  313:     mkdirSync(p, { recursive: true })
  314:   }
  315:   return created
  316: }

  318: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  319: // Content scaffolding
  320: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  321: 
  322: export function scaffoldContentFiles(
  323:   paths: ScaffoldPaths,
  324:   templates: string,
  325:   goalSummary: string,
  326:   projectName: string,
  327:   force: boolean,
  328: ): { created: string[]; preserved: string[] } {
  329:   const created: string[] = []
  330:   const preserved: string[] = []
  331: 
  332:   const pairs: Array<[string, string, Record<string, string>]> = [
  333:     [
  334:       join(templates, 'goal.md'),
  335:       paths.goalPath,
  336:       {
  337:         GOAL_VERSION: paths.goalVersion,
  338:         DATE: paths.dateLabel,
  339:         PREVIOUS_VERSION: paths.previousVersion,
  340:         GOAL_SUMMARY: goalSummary.trim(),
  341:       },
  342:     ],
  343:     [
  344:       join(templates, 'todo.md'),
  345:       paths.todoPath,
  346:       {
  347:         GOAL_VERSION: paths.goalVersion,
  348:         TODO_SUBVERSION: paths.todoVersion,
  349:         DATE: paths.dateLabel,
  350:       },
  351:     ],
  352:     [
  353:       join(templates, 'chat-log.md'),
  354:       paths.chatPath,
  355:       {
  356:         DATE: paths.dateLabel,
  357:         TIME: paths.timeLabel,
  358:         PROJECT_NAME: projectName.trim(),
  359:       },
  360:     ],
  361:   ]
  362: 
  363:   for (const [templatePath, targetPath, values] of pairs) {
  364:     if (writeTemplate(templatePath, targetPath, force, values)) {
  365:       created.push(toPosixPath(relative(paths.root, targetPath)))
  366:     } else {
  367:       preserved.push(toPosixPath(relative(paths.root, targetPath)))
  368:     }
  369:   }
  370: 
  371:   return { created, preserved }
  372: }

  376: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  377: 
  378: function isFile(p: string): boolean {
  379:   try { return statSync(p).isFile() } catch { return false }
  380: }

  201: 
  202: export function upgrade(
  203:   root: string,
  204:   skillRoot: string,
  205:   templates: string,
  206:   effectiveLocale: string,
  207:   requestedLocale: string,
  208:   goalSummary: string,
  209:   projectName: string,
  210:   dt: Date,
  211: ): UpgradeResult {
  212:   const initialState = detectState(root)
  213: 
  214:   const createdDirs = createMemoryDirs(root)
  215:   const paths = resolveScaffoldPaths(root, dt)
  216:   const { created: createdFiles, preserved: preservedFiles } = scaffoldContentFiles(
  217:     paths, templates, goalSummary, projectName, false,
  218:   )
  219: 
  220:   const agentsPath = join(root, 'AGENTS.md')
  221:   let agentsAction = 'preserved_existing'
  222:   if (!existsSync(agentsPath)) {
  223:     const agentsTemplate = join(templates, 'agents.md')
  224:     if (existsSync(agentsTemplate) && writeTemplate(agentsTemplate, agentsPath, false, {})) {
  225:       createdFiles.push('AGENTS.md')
  226:       agentsAction = 'created'
  227:     }
  228:   } else {
  229:     preservedFiles.push('AGENTS.md')
  230:   }
  231:   const mergeRequired = agentsAction === 'preserved_existing' && needsAgentsMerge(agentsPath)
  232: 
  233:   return {
  234:     state_before: initialState,
  235:     state_after: detectState(root),
  236:     requested_locale: requestedLocale,
  237:     effective_locale: effectiveLocale,
  238:     created_dirs: createdDirs,
  239:     created_files: createdFiles,
  240:     preserved_files: preservedFiles,
  241:     agents_action: agentsAction,
  242:     agents_merge_required: mergeRequired,
  243:   }
  244: }
  245: 
  246: export function formatResultText(result: UpgradeResult): string {
  247:   const lines = [
  248:     `state_before: ${result.state_before}`,
  249:     `state_after: ${result.state_after}`,
  250:     `effective_locale: ${result.effective_locale}`,
  251:     `agents_action: ${result.agents_action}`,
  252:     `agents_merge_required: ${result.agents_merge_required}`,
  253:   ]
  254:   if (result.created_dirs.length > 0) {
  255:     lines.push('created_dirs:')
  256:     for (const item of result.created_dirs) lines.push(`- ${item}`)
  257:   }
  258:   if (result.created_files.length > 0) {
  259:     lines.push('created_files:')
  260:     for (const item of result.created_files) lines.push(`- ${item}`)
  261:   }
  262:   if (result.preserved_files.length > 0) {
  263:     lines.push('preserved_files:')
  264:     for (const item of result.preserved_files) lines.push(`- ${item}`)
  265:   }
  266:   return lines.join('\n')
  267: }

  290: function loadManifestsRecursive(dir: string, out: ManifestEntry[]): void {

  315:     } else {

  322: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  325: 
  326: /** Parse messages from a clean markdown file. */
  327: export function parseCleanMarkdownMessages(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): RenderedMessage[] {
  328:   const repoPath = m.repo_clean_path ? join(root, m.repo_clean_path) : null
  329:   const cleanPath = (repoPath && existsSync(repoPath)) ? repoPath : m.global_clean_path
  330:   if (!cleanPath || !existsSync(cleanPath)) return []
  331: 
  332:   let content: string
  333:   try {
  334:     content = readFileSync(cleanPath, 'utf-8')
  335:   } catch {
  336:     return []
  337:   }
  338: 
  339:   return parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content)
  340: }
  341: 
  342: /** Extract role/timestamp/text from clean markdown format. */
  343: export function parseMessagesFromMarkdown(content: string): RenderedMessage[] {
  344:   const messages: RenderedMessage[] = []
  345: 
  346:   // Find ## Messages section
  347:   const msgSection = content.indexOf('\n## Messages')
  348:   if (msgSection === -1) return []
  349: 
  350:   const body = content.slice(msgSection + 12) // after "## Messages\n"
  351: 
  352:   // Split on ### N. role headers
  353:   const msgRe = /^### \d+\.\s+(\w+)/gm
  354:   let match: RegExpExecArray | null
  355:   const starts: Array<{ index: number; role: string }> = []
  356: 
  357:   while ((match = msgRe.exec(body)) !== null) {
  358:     starts.push({ index: match.index, role: match[1] ?? 'unknown' })
  359:   }
  360: 
  361:   for (let i = 0; i < starts.length; i++) {
  362:     const start = starts[i]
  363:     if (!start) continue
  364:     const nextStart = starts[i + 1]
  365:     const chunk = nextStart
  366:       ? body.slice(start.index, nextStart.index)
  367:       : body.slice(start.index)
  368: 
  369:     const lines = chunk.split('\n')
  370:     // First line is the header, second may be timestamp
  371:     let timestamp = ''
  372:     const textLines: string[] = []
  373:     let pastHeader = false
  374: 
  375:     for (const line of lines) {
  376:       if (!pastHeader && line.startsWith('### ')) {
  377:         pastHeader = true
  378:         continue
  379:       }
  380:       if (!pastHeader) continue
  381:       const tsMatch = line.match(/^- Timestamp:\s*`([^`]+)`/)
  382:       if (tsMatch && !timestamp) {
  383:         timestamp = tsMatch[1] ?? ''
  384:         continue
  385:       }
  386:       textLines.push(line)
  387:     }
  388: 
  389:     // Remove leading/trailing blank lines from text
  390:     const text = textLines.join('\n').trim()
  391:     if (text || timestamp) {
  392:       messages.push({ role: start.role, timestamp, text })
  393:     }
  394:   }
  395: 
  396:   return messages
  397: }

  403: /** Best-effort extraction of tool event summaries from a raw JSONL file. */

  405:   const summaries: string[] = []

  417:       const record = JSON.parse(trimmed) as Record<string, unknown>
  418:       // Codex: function_call type

  421:         const name = (record['name'] as string | undefined) ?? type
  422:         summaries.push(name)
  423:         continue
  424:       }
  425:       // Claude: tool_use content blocks
  426:       const payload = record['message'] as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
  427:       const content2 = (payload?.['content'] ?? record['content']) as unknown[] | undefined
  428:       if (Array.isArray(content2)) {
  429:         for (const block of content2) {
  430:           if (
  431:             block !== null &&
  432:             typeof block === 'object' &&
  433:             (block as Record<string, unknown>)['type'] === 'tool_use'
  434:           ) {
  435:             const name = ((block as Record<string, unknown>)['name'] as string | undefined) ?? 'tool_use'
  436:             summaries.push(name)
  437:           }
  438:         }
  439:       }
  440:     } catch {
  441:       // Skip malformed lines
  442:     }
  443:   }
  444: 
  445:   return summaries
  446: }
  447: 
  448: function extractToolNamesFromEvents(events: TranscriptEvent[]): string[] {
  449:   return events
  450:     .filter((event): event is Extract<TranscriptEvent, { kind: 'tool_call' }> => event.kind === 'tool_call')
  451:     .map(event => event.tool_name)

  454: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  458: function transcriptOutputPath(output: string, m: ManifestEntry): string {

  461:   return join(output, 'transcripts', m.client, `${stem}.html`)

  463: 
  464: function resolveRawPath(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): string | null {
  465:   if (m.repo_raw_path) {
  466:     const repoPath = join(root, m.repo_raw_path)
  467:     if (existsSync(repoPath)) return repoPath
  468:   }
  469:   if (m.global_raw_path) {
  470:     return m.global_raw_path
  471:   }

  473: }
  474: 
  475: function resolveFullPath(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): string | null {
  476:   if (m.repo_full_path) {
  477:     const repoPath = join(root, m.repo_full_path)
  478:     if (existsSync(repoPath)) return repoPath
  479:   }
  480:   if (m.global_full_path && existsSync(m.global_full_path)) {
  481:     return m.global_full_path
  482:   }
  483:   const derivedRepoPath = deriveFullPathFromCleanPath(m.repo_clean_path ? join(root, m.repo_clean_path) : '')
  484:   if (derivedRepoPath) {
  485:     return derivedRepoPath
  486:   }
  487:   const derivedGlobalPath = deriveFullPathFromCleanPath(m.global_clean_path)

  489:     return derivedGlobalPath

  491:   return null

  493: 
  494: function loadNormalizedTranscript(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): ParsedTranscript | null {
  495:   const fullPath = resolveFullPath(m, root)
  496:   if (!fullPath) return null
  497: 
  498:   try {
  499:     const raw = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')
  500:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Record<string, unknown>
  501:     if (
  502:       !Array.isArray(parsed['messages']) ||
  503:       !Array.isArray(parsed['tool_events']) ||
  504:       !Array.isArray(parsed['events'])
  505:     ) {
  506:       return null
  507:     }
  508: 
  509:     return {
  510:       client: String(parsed['client'] ?? m.client) as ParsedTranscript['client'],
  511:       session_id: String(parsed['session_id'] ?? m.session_id),
  512:       title: String(parsed['title'] ?? m.title),
  513:       started_at: String(parsed['started_at'] ?? m.started_at),
  514:       cwd: String(parsed['cwd'] ?? m.cwd),
  515:       branch: String(parsed['branch'] ?? m.branch),
  516:       messages: parsed['messages'] as ParsedTranscript['messages'],
  517:       tool_events: parsed['tool_events'] as ParsedTranscript['tool_events'],
  518:       events: parsed['events'] as ParsedTranscript['events'],
  519:       source_path: String(parsed['source_path'] ?? m.raw_source_path),
  520:     }

  522:     return null
  523:   }

  526: function loadStructuredTranscript(m: ManifestEntry, root: string): ParsedTranscript | null {

  540:     return null
  541:   }

  543: 
  544: function deriveFullPathFromCleanPath(cleanPath: string): string | null {
  545:   if (!cleanPath || !existsSync(cleanPath)) return null
  546:   const fullPath = cleanPath
  547:     .replace(/[\\/]clean[\\/]/, '/full/')
  548:     .replace(/\.md$/i, '.json')
  549:   return existsSync(fullPath) ? fullPath : null

  551: 
  552: function toRenderedMessage(message: ParsedTranscript['messages'][number]): RenderedMessage {
  553:   return {
  554:     role: message.role,
  555:     timestamp: message.timestamp ?? '',
  556:     text: message.text,
  557:   }
  558: }
  559: 
  560: function snippetFromEvents(events: TranscriptEvent[]): string {
  561:   for (const event of events) {
  562:     if (event.kind === 'message') {
  563:       return event.text.slice(0, 300)
  564:     }
  565:     if (event.kind === 'reasoning') {
  566:       const text = event.text || event.summary || ''
  567:       if (text) return text.slice(0, 300)
  568:     }
  569:     if (event.summary) {
  570:       return event.summary.slice(0, 300)
  571:     }
  572:   }
  573:   return ''
  574: }
  575: 
  576: function buildSearchText(messages: RenderedMessage[], events: TranscriptEvent[]): string {
  577:   const headParts: string[] = []
  578:   const bodyParts: string[] = []
  579: 
  580:   if (events.length > 0) {
  581:     for (const event of events) {
  582:       if (event.kind === 'message') {
  583:         bodyParts.push(event.role, event.text)
  584:         continue
  585:       }
  586:       if (event.kind === 'reasoning') {
  587:         headParts.push(event.text || event.summary || 'reasoning')
  588:         continue
  589:       }
  590:       if (event.kind === 'tool_call' || event.kind === 'tool_result') {
  591:         headParts.push(event.tool_name, event.summary || '')
  592:         continue
  593:       }
  594:       if (event.title) {
  595:         headParts.push(event.title)
  596:       }
  597:       if (event.summary) {
  598:         headParts.push(event.summary)
  599:       }
  600:     }
  601:   } else {
  602:     for (const message of messages) {
  603:       bodyParts.push(message.role, message.text)
  604:     }
  605:   }
  606: 
  607:   return normalizeSearchText([...headParts, ...bodyParts].join('\n')).slice(0, 1600)
  608: }
  609: 
  610: function normalizeSearchText(text: string): string {
  611:   return text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim()
  612: }

  618: function loadMarkdownFiles(dir: string): Array<{ filename: string; title: string; content: string }> {
  619:   if (!existsSync(dir)) return []
  620: 
  621:   let entries: string[]
  622:   try {
  623:     entries = readdirSync(dir)
  624:   } catch {
  625:     return []
  626:   }
  627: 
  628:   return entries
  629:     .filter(e => e.endsWith('.md'))
  630:     .sort()
  631:     .map(filename => {
  632:       const fullPath = join(dir, filename)
  633:       let content = ''
  634:       try {

  636:       } catch {
  637:         // empty
  638:       }
  639:       const title = extractMarkdownTitle(content) || basename(filename, '.md')
  640:       return { filename, title, content }
  641:     })
  642: }
  643: 
  644: function clearOutputDir(output: string): void {
  645:   if (!existsSync(output)) return
  646: 
  647:   let entries: string[]
  648:   try {
  649:     entries = readdirSync(output)
  650:   } catch {
  651:     return
  652:   }
  653: 
  654:   for (const entry of entries) {
  655:     try {
  656:       rmSync(join(output, entry), { recursive: true, force: true })
  657:     } catch {
  658:       // Best-effort cleanup
  659:     }
  660:   }
  661: }
  662: 
  663: function extractMarkdownTitle(content: string): string {
  664:   const match = content.match(/^#\s+(.+)$/m)
  665:   return match?.[1]?.trim() ?? ''
  666: }
